rationale: Three targeted bugfixes in a Superset fork. (1) superset/db_engine_specs/bigquery.py replaces the simple super().fetch_data() with a progressive-fetch implementation that fetches an initial sample (_BQ_INITIAL_SAMPLE_ROWS = 1000) via cursor.fetchmany, estimates per-row bytes with sys.getsizeof(row)+sum(sys.getsizeof(v) for v in row), extrapolates max_rows = max_bytes / avg_bytes_per_row against config['BQ_FETCH_MAX_MB'] (default 200 in config.py), then fetches one extra row to detect actual truncation, and sets `g.bq_memory_limited`/`g.bq_memory_limited_row_count` for downstream surfacing. The broad except wraps the size-estimation path in a documented degradation-to-parent — that's a legitimate fallback (data is still returned), not an error-swallow cheat. (2) superset/db_engine_specs/base.py wraps SQLScript(sql, engine).statements[-1].get_limit_value() in try/except SupersetParseError → returns None for malformed `LIMIT` clauses that sqlglot 30+ can't parse, with a comment explaining the reason. (3) superset-frontend/src/chartCustomizations/components/{DynamicGroupBy,TimeColumn,TimeGrain}/controlPanel.ts converts `label: t('...')` / `description: t('...')` to `label: () => t('...')` / `description: () => t('...')` so translations are resolved at render time instead of module-load time; three new controlPanel.test.ts files use a collectFunctionProps helper to assert every label/description function returns a string. The cache-manager and schemas.py persist/expose `bq_memory_limited` so the warning survives cache hits and is propagated as `warning` in ChartDataResponseResult. The PR was closed without merge but the diff is a legitimate, well-tested bugfix set.
